query: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis latest status July 2026 - are attacks on ships continuing or has there been a ceasefire/truce? UKMTO attack incidents in Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Gulf of Oman in July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

## Key Finding

**As of July 14, 2026, there is NO ceasefire — the truce collapsed and ship attacks are actively continuing.**

The interim US–Iran truce (the "Islamabad Memorandum" MOU signed June 17) **broke down on July 8, 2026** after Iran allegedly struck multiple commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The situation has since escalated into open military conflict:

- **Ship attacks continuing:** Iran struck 3 commercial vessels July 7 (incl. Qatari LNG tanker *al-Rakiyat*); IRGC claimed striking 2 more tankers July 13; trackers report additional tanker hits July 14.
- **Three nights of US strikes (July 11–13):** including a 140-target package on July 11 — the largest of the conflict — plus a July 9 strike on the Bushehr nuclear plant perimeter (14 killed, 78 wounded).
- **Iranian retaliation** against Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Jordan, UAE, and even mediator Oman.
- **Strait effectively closed** (Day ~135): only ~10 vessels transited July 12 (11% of normal ~88/day).
- **Markets:** Brent ~$84.61 (+7% in 24h); war-risk insurance ~8× normal; crisis pressure at "extreme" (91/100).

**Caveat noted in the report:** CNN pages were geoblocked (451 errors) so I relied on their search snippets; some July 14 casualty specifics come from aggregator trackers (straits.live, hormuzstraitmonitor.com) and should be treated as preliminary — though the core facts (truce collapse, ongoing attacks, three nights of strikes) are corroborated across Wikipedia, Al Jazeera, CNN, and UN News. UKMTO's own recent-incidents page returned 0 reports at access time.

Full findings with all sources and data points have been submitted via the structured output.
